name: Ludus Latrunculorum
author: Traditional (Roman); reconstruction Ulrich Schädler / Locus Ludi; module by Abstract Games factory
description: The Roman 'game of little soldiers', as reconstructed by Ulrich Schädler and published in playable form by the Locus Ludi project. Players first drop all their counters on an empty 8×8 board, then step them orthogonally (Seneca variant: also draughts-style leaps over own counters), capturing by enclosing an enemy counter between two of their own. In the Seneca variant a surrounded counter is first immobilised ('incitus') and only removed at the start of the captor's next turn — and is set free if one of its two guards is itself trapped. Most captures wins.
